critical_issues.issue: 66% workflow failure rate (34% success) across 20 workflows with 3 critical production pipelines failing
critical_issues.root_cause: Critical path workflows (Deploy Site to Netlify, james_butler_daily.yml, pages deployment) are failing repeatedly. Pattern indicates either: (1) dependency version conflicts causing build breaks, (2) authentication tokens expired/misconfigured for deployment services, or (3) rate limits being hit on scheduled workflows causing cascading failures

fixes.action: Audit and rotate all deployment credentials (Netlify token, GitHub Pages token, API keys in james_butler_daily.yml)
fixes.implementation: 1. Check Netlify deploy logs for 401/403 errors. 2. Regenerate Netlify API token in account settings. 3. Update NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN in GitHub repo secrets. 4. Verify GitHub Pages deployment token hasn't expired. 5. Re-run failed workflows to confirm auth fix. 6. Document token expiration dates in runbook.

fixes.action: Add explicit dependency pinning and caching to all 3 critical workflows
fixes.implementation: 1. Pin exact versions (not ^/~) in package.json for Deploy Site workflow. 2. Add actions/cache@v3 to cache node_modules with hash key. 3. Pin GitHub Actions versions to commit SHAs (not @v2/@v3 tags). 4. Add timeout-minutes: 15 to prevent hung jobs. 5. Enable verbose logging (--verbose flag) to catch silent failures. 6. Test on staging branch first.

fixes.action: Implement workflow failure alerting and automatic retry with exponential backoff
fixes.implementation: 1. Add on-failure webhook to Slack/email for critical workflows. 2. Wrap james_butler_daily.yml job in retry action (nick-invision/retry@v2, max_attempts: 3, timeout_minutes: 10). 3. Add conditional retry only for rate-limit/network errors (exit codes 2, 124). 4. Space out scheduled workflows by 15+ min to avoid GitHub rate limits. 5. Add workflow_dispatch trigger for manual re-runs.

critical_issues.success_metric: workflows.success_rate increases from 34% to >85% within 7 days; zero consecutive failures on critical workflows over 48-hour period
anti_patterns: Do NOT just re-run failed workflows without checking logs first - this wastes Actions minutes and masks root cause
Do NOT upgrade all dependencies at once - changes must be isolated to identify what breaks
Do NOT ignore rate limit errors (403) - GitHub has strict API limits on scheduled workflows; must add delays
Do NOT use floating version tags (@latest, @v2) - these cause non-deterministic failures when upstream breaks

wins_to_amplify: 20 workflows deployed shows strong automation culture - preserve this velocity while fixing stability
Critical workflows are correctly identified and separated - leverage this classification to add priority routing
learning_insights: 34% success rate means 13 of 20 workflows are failing - this is beyond isolated incidents and indicates systemic config/auth issue
Three named critical workflows suggests these are customer-facing - every failure directly impacts site availability
james_butler_daily.yml name pattern indicates scheduled job - likely hitting rate limits or timezone-based token expiration
No error details in report means monitoring needs instrumentation layer - add structured logging before next diagnostic cycle
